locations.commerce: Import: Foodstuffs, Industrial Goods, Electronic Goods. - Export: Commercial Goods, Advanced Technology
locations.description: Background: Stardust City (or just “the City”, to locals) is a gleaming beacon of progress. It was originally the crown city of the Stardust Empire, and became the hometown of Stardust, Inc. when it was founded. A sprawling metropolis running entirely on Crystal power, Stardust City has been the seat of power of Stardust, Inc. since its founding, and it makes sure no one forgets it. From food to sanitation, communications and construction, everything is owned and run by Stardust, Inc.. THORN also has a significant presence here, as the best place to fight is in the core of the enemy’s territory. Landscape: The first things anyone thinks of when Stardust CIty is mentioned are the gleaming skyscrapers and organised streets. The centre of the city is laid out in a neat grid, making it very easy to navigate. The city also boasts the most recognisable skyline in the world, with the cluster of towering skyscrapers at its heart dwarfed by the ring of Crystal power generators spaced around the city’s original boundary. As time passed, the city grew, but the construction standards were not maintained beyond the ring of power generators. The result is that, rather than being akin to suburbs, the outer areas resemble slums. These areas house people with just enough worth to Stardust that they are allowed to remain, commuting to work inside the city proper. The night in Stardust City glows a cyan colour with the light pollution from the huge number of Crystals housed within the great power plants, making the city seem to glow as if it is made of stardust. Population: Stardust, Inc. does not allow professional discrimination, so all races can be found alongside each other in its home city. There is, however, a slight imbalance toward the faster-reproducing Sen, which is almost inevitable over a period of time. As the corporation owns the city, all available jobs relate to it in some way, whether it’s working as accountants, engineers or researchers for Stardust, Inc. directly, or providing services to employees, such as shopkeepers and couriers. Laws: The laws of Stardust City have been revised from what was in place when Stardust was an empire, with most being lifted directly out of the corporation’s handbook rather than designed for the people. In addition to “common sense” laws like those relating to violence and theft, there are surprisingly large penalties relating to day-to-day conduct and presentation. Vandalism, public disorder and similar infractions are viewed as serious crimes by Stardust, Inc., as it is very focused on its image. It therefore punishes them more severely than most places in the world. The laws are enforced by the Stardust robots which roam the streets. Although they are officially overseen by Warrior-Monks, these figureheads have no real say in how justice is dispensed. Instead, the Peacekeeping Division of Stardust, Inc. makes all of the decisions. Dissenters say that the number of robots used to police the city is excessive – tens of villages could be protected if the robot presence at the centre were reduced. Stardust, Inc. laims that, for as long as groups like THORN subvert the law, the level of protection provided by the robots is needed to keep the population safe.
An elven ranger with a keen eye and a mysterious past.
